Roblox's vision is to reimagine the way people come together. Our mission is to connect a billion people with optimism and civility.

What is Roblox?

Roblox is a platform where people come together virtually to share experiences. Every day tens of millions of people from around the world come to Roblox to learn work play connect communicate and socialize in immersive digital experiences all built by a global community of creators.

Powered by Creators

Roblox is powered by a global community of millions of developers and creators who produce their own immersive multiplayer experiences each month using Roblox Studio our intuitive desktop design tool. Any experience imaginable can be created on Roblox.

How Popular?

Roblox is ranked as one of the top entertainment platforms for audiences under the age of 18 based on average monthly visits and time spent (Comscore). Our popularity is driven purely by the community with millions of people signing up through word of mouth every month.

about:
I build and invest in sustainable profitable high-growth businesses. I'm a technical founder. I believe in solving big problems where untapped opportunities and inefficiencies exist.

As an entrepreneur I've created companies that I've wanted to use myself and that I believed should exist. As an investor I don't look at what's hot now or what has worked in the past. Instead I imagine: "What do I want the world to be like 10 years from now? What kind of place do I want to help create for today's youth to inherit tomorrow?"

I founded SysOpt.com (editorial hardware reviews) and ResellerRatings (one of the first ratings web sites) in high school ('96) with no funding or debt sold both to EarthWeb ('99) for $4M and repurchased ResellerRatings ('02) for $32k. I grew and then sold ResellerRatings again in 2012 this time to Answers.com for $34M. As the sole 100% equity holder of this bootstrapped business the return on capital between 2002 and 2012 was 1 000x.

In 2002 I rebuilt ResellerRatings hired and grew the team to scale the business from $0 in 2002 to a highly profitable $6M/year of recurring subscription revenue in 2012 and sold the business in 2012 to Answers. Post acquisition as GM I grew revenue by 70%+ over the next year and led the business to 2 000 concurrent paid subscribers including Home Depot Wayfair L'Occitane Newegg B&H Photo and Vitacost. I also secured multi-year ratings data licenses with Google and Bing adding significant revenue.

*TechCrunch: ResellerRatings was acquired by Answers:
http://goo.gl/ur0HO

More recently I founded Fareness a flight deals app that lets consumers find the cheapest dates and places for their next trip at a glance without the usual hours of searching. Fareness' main goal is to make the life changing experience of travel accessible to more people.

Current areas of investment: sustainable transportation aerospace travel and hospitality energy efficiency early disease detection.

Specialties: Travel search SaaS / cloud based B2B subscriptions ratings and reviews of merchants online retail social commerce Internet marketing and advertising M&A contract negotiation licensing software development eCommerce user generated content (UGC) and community.
current_position_description: Fareness makes it effortless to find and book flights on the least expensive dates and to compare the lowest prices across themed destinations (like beaches Asia or Europe).
current_position_2_description: Dealighted finds over 1 000 daily deals on products posted by users from sites like Slickdeals Fatwallet Anandtech and others and highlights the best 100 using social signals and machine learning.
company_description:
Fareness set out to organize the world’s airfares enabling consumers to simultaneously browse the lowest priced round trip fares for 190+ departure dates to global destinations with a single sub-second search paired with an ideal hotel in just the right place.

The best way to think about the power of Fareness is to imagine trying to find the lowest priced flight from San Francisco to Paris or Rome in September or October for 7 8 or 9 days: you'd need to run 360 searches manually (SFO->Paris 9/1-9/8 9/2-9/9 SFO->Rome 9/1-9/9 9/2-9/10 etc) to compare all destination and date combinations since there are a huge number of factors that affect each round trip price. With Fareness all of this is accomplished with one search in about 1 second.

Want to fly to Paris for a 7 day trip sometime in August? Fareness will give you the lowest priced flight but you might change your mind if you see that Rome is $300 less per person. Want to travel to the beach sometime in September for 6 days? We’ll give you the lowest priced round trip September flights from your home airport to Maui Honolulu Cabo Cancun and the Bahamas all at a glance with one search.

This is airfare comparison the way it should be — it's the missing link to being fully informed about fare options when planning and booking a trip.

company_description: Mammoth Biosciences is a biotechnology company focused on leveraging its proprietary ultracompact CRISPR systems to develop long-term curative therapies as well as other applications such as decentralized precision diagnostics. about:
In the last decade my career has been obsessively focused on putting customers at the heart of everything. As CEO of LogicMonitor I am focused on accelerating our growth and leadership within the rapidly evolving IT observability space ensuring that technology and business goals are seamlessly aligned.

My foundation is built on scaling high-growth SaaS organizations. I was a founding member of the Slack Enterprise team where I grew revenue from $90M to over $1B making it the fastest enterprise software company to reach that milestone. During my 15-year tenure at Salesforce I led the transformation of global renewal businesses and built modern customer success models from the ground up.

Beyond operations I am passionate about corporate governance and investing. I currently serve on the boards of Nasuni Northwestern Board of Trustees and I am a Founding LP at Operator Collective. My goal is always the same: helping organizations navigate pivots and rapid growth while keeping the human element—the customer—at the center.
company_description:
We're in the midst of an information revolution and monitoring is at the center of it. Businesses are moving from asking "What happened?" to predicting what's coming solving problems before they start and using data to unlock opportunities. 

At LogicMonitor we're committed to expanding what's possible for businesses by advancing their technology. After all monitoring shouldn't just help businesses see what's in front of them—it should create new ways for them to grow. Here's how we live our vision:

Turn It On - Today monitoring keeps the world of business turning. LogicMonitor comprehensively monitors infrastructures from on-premises to the cloud. Within minutes we make it possible for businesses to advance their technology ensuring airline passengers aren't stranded due to system outages food stays at safe temperatures and spinning wheels don't interrupt our favorite streaming shows.

Turn the Dial - We believe monitoring should do more than visualize today's information—it should open up new ways for businesses to grow. We go above and beyond to empower our customers seeking to be the most extensible monitoring platform in the world. When our customers can solve problems before they start they can move their focus from maintenance to innovation.

Turn the Corner - As artificial intelligence and machine learning turn from sci-fi promises to everyday realities our technology is enabling businesses to see what's coming before it happens. We collaborate closely with our customers to understand their risks and anticipate their needs providing insights that unlock their vision.

Our company has a global presence with offices in Santa Barbara Austin London India Australia and Singapore.
headline: CEO Board Director CCO Investor LogicMonitor Slack Salesforce

Christine Heckart is the founder and CEO of Xapa World and has experience on both sides of the board table. She brings over thirty years of experience in the technology industry having held a variety of leadership roles including CEO GM president and CMO in industries that include entertainment SaaS data analytics security networking and storage. She has worked in B2C and B2B business models at companies ranging in size from one person to over one hundred thousand at iconic brands such as Microsoft Cisco Systems and NetApp. Having begun in new product development Heckart's passion is in forging wholly new markets and she has launched three new categories that have reached several billion in revenues annually. She has also created disruptive business models and worked through big market downturns and inflections. 

Heckart founded Xapa with the intent of helping to scale leadership development to all levels of an organization and beyond to society as a whole. Xapa is a gamified app that helps people learn and practice critical skills such as self awareness growth mindset and adaptability self care communication and trust management and influence and more. It is intended to help create cultures of high performance accountability innovation and collaboration. 

Heckart is known for her strategic vision and understanding of market transitions. She has experience in high growth environments where scale challenges predominate and is also familiar with the challenges of leading major transformational change. Recently named one of the Fifty Most Powerful Women in Technology by the National Diversity Council and a 2016 Woman of Influence by Silicon Valley Business Journal her passion is helping people and companies achieve more than they ever thought possible.

Heckart serves on the board of directors for SiTime and Contentful and previously for Lam Research Scalyr and 6Sense. She teaches board governance and sits on the board for Berkeley's Next Gen Director's Adademy.

Married for 35 years with three grown children Christine is a agender who writes paints and has followed and studied quantum mechanics cosmology and archaeology for the past 30 years. She graduated Magna Cum Laude and Phi Beta Kappa with a degree in Economics from the University of Colorado Boulder.

I care less about whether you use physics robotics AI bio or chem and more about the (scale of the) problem being solved with the tech. Over the course of my career I have seen over 200 transactions -- spanning company formation to growth acquisition and special & strategic situations -- spread over three funds ($150M $277M and $306M) in critical industries such as AI x therapeutics healthcare agriculture AI reasoning and frontier models industrial productivity and beyond!

I work in high-resolution with all my investments rolling up my sleeves to deliver 0-to-1 step changes shaping corporate development strategy and originating high-stakes commercial bets and non-standard ecosystem partnerships. Observing recurring dichotomies the hard decisions and human costs in the trenches of company building have been instrumental in defining my investment and operational lenses.

It is my core belief that deep tech and the emerging frontier of embodied AI will redefine humanity's means of production and distribution. Accelerating tech adoption curves give me hope that deep tech will diffuse rapidly in emerging markets deepening economic and human development globally. I consider myself a diplomat and not a disruptive innovator: In my view reaching national-scale manufacturing mandates fostering innovation ecosystems and supercharging discoveries requires academia startups corporates and governments to work together and to integrate with installed infrastructure to advocate for policy reform. 

The future is our friend!

Projects and startups emerging from the program have touched the lives of more than 180 million people so far.

Stuart is an investor in and advisor to early stage technology and social impact organizations in Silicon Valley and his native Ireland. He is a member of Trinity College Dublin’s Provost’s Council. He also grows old-vine Zinfandel grapes in California's Russian River Valley and enjoys regularly quality testing the resulting award-winning wines.

Stuart’s background is in software development data communications and travel reservations systems. He co-founded Gradient Solutions a pioneer in web-based travel e-commerce acquired by Sabre.

Stuart holds a BA (Mod.) in Computer Science from Trinity College Dublin an MBA from the University of Geneva and an EMBA from the Tepper Business School Carnegie Mellon University.

about:
Victor W. Hwang is an economic growth expert. His ideas have shaped the economic lives of millions of people worldwide. His work has helped over 300 communities cities states companies and entire countries create greater prosperity. 

Victor’s path was inspired by his parents as educators who had to start a business to afford sending him to Harvard. But after the first day of Economics at Harvard Victor left the class and never returned. He realized that Economics – the way it was taught at elite institutions – was flawed. It didn’t account for his parents and all the ordinary people like them trying to make their way. Victor’s life’s journey followed from that day. He searched for answers to the questions “How can we create prosperity by taking into account everyday people? How do we make a world that doesn’t stand in their way?”

Victor became an entrepreneur and started several companies organizations and initiatives fighting to remove barriers holding people back. Previously he was Vice President of Entrepreneurship at the Kauffman Foundation the world’s leading philanthropy supporting entrepreneurs with an endowment of $2 billion.
 
Victor pioneered the concept of “rainforest” ecosystems in a business context by showing how communities can replicate the historical innovation dynamics of Silicon Valley. His book The Rainforest was awarded Book of the Year Gold Medal by ForeWord Reviews for “a big idea that defines a way of thinking.” 

National Public Radio named Victor's graduation address to Austin Community College one of “the best commencement speeches ever.” 

For more info visit http://www.victorh.co

about: When she was 8 Laura Deming realized that we were all going to die of a disease called aging. Ever since her driving passion has been to slow aging and eliminate age-related disease. She started working in a biogerontology lab when she was 12 and matriculated at MIT when she was 14. At 17 she was one of the youngest 20under20 fellows awarded $100 000 by Peter Thiel to pursue her venture full time. Laura is currently a full-time partner at The Longevity Fund an early stage venture capital fund backing companies which target the aging process to treat disease with investments in gene editing small molecule therapeutics and novel methods to treat disease.
company_description:
We are building reversible cryopreservation technology to solve the organ procurement and matching problem.

Cryopreservation is already essential to modern medicine — powering IVF and life-saving stem cell therapies. 

Now we're extending this technology to whole human organs — transforming hours of viability into weeks or months however long is necessary — so that no donated organ is lost to logistics and no patient dies waiting. 

By solving this immediate need we de-risk and build the foundation for what comes next: whole-body reversible cryopreservation. We're pausing molecular motion to give patients a bridge to future cures.
headline: CEO at Until

about:
Will Weisman is the Founder and Managing Partner of KittyHawk Ventures an investment firm focused on backing extraordinary mission-driven companies from seed through late-stage pre-IPO.

KittyHawk’s flagship late-stage strategy KittyHawk Nasdaq Top 20 is a first-of-its-kind private markets index fund developed in partnership with Nasdaq and Nasdaq Private Market. The strategy provides structured exposure to the 20 largest and most liquid private companies globally combining index discipline with active execution to access companies where the majority of value creation now occurs prior to IPO.

Complementing this strategy KittyHawk Frontier focuses on leading early-stage investments in breakthrough technologies across AI healthcare energy aerospace infrastructure and advanced materials. Together the firm’s strategies reflect a full lifecycle approach to investing at the frontiers of innovation—from company formation through scaled market leadership.

The firm takes its name from Kitty Hawk North Carolina where the Wright Brothers first achieved powered flight—an enduring symbol of what becomes possible when vision meets persistence. In that spirit KittyHawk partners with founders building “N-of-1” companies capable of defining entirely new categories.

Prior to founding KittyHawk Will spent seven years as an Executive Director at Singularity University founded by Ray Kurzweil and Peter Diamandis. During his tenure he helped scale the organization into a global benefit corporation with a presence in over 150 countries and a community of hundreds of thousands of entrepreneurs executives and innovators. He also served as Executive Director of Peter Diamandis' Abundance360 working closely with CEOs and global leaders navigating exponential technological change.

Earlier in his career Will founded World Wrapps a fast-casual restaurant concept that invented and then helped popularize the wrap as a mainstream food category growing the business to over 30 locations before its sale. 

Will holds an MBA from the Stanford Graduate School of Business and a BA from the University of California Santa Barbara. He is based in Los Angeles.
company_description:
KittyHawk an investment firm focused on leading edge seed to late stage pre-IPO companies.

The firm is named after Kitty Hawk North Carolina where the Wright Brothers embarked on an extraordinary entrepreneurial journey creating the world's first airplane and launching a new era in human mobility.

KittyHawk supports extraordinary mission driven entrepreneurs in their quest to build world changing organizations. The firm operates two primary strategies: 

KittyHawk Frontier focuses on seed stage investments at the frontiers of technology including AI Quantum Aerospace Healthcare Longevity Climate Web 3 and more.

KittyHawk Secondaries are pre-IPO secondary funds that leverage a deep relationship with Nasdaq Private Markets to access investment opportunities in the world's leading late-stage companies.

The firm was founded in 2015 and is run by former Singularity University Executive Director Will Weisman.

about:
“All things being equal take the path of greatest adventure” is my mantra as I help innovative leaders navigate the frontier. 

With three decades of operating experience in startups and complex enterprises I've advised hundreds of executives faculty creatives and founders who have raised more than $1B in venture capital. I've been recognized by Rolling Stone WIRED Forbes and Entrepreneur Mag for my ability to scout untapped innovators and devise novel business models for global impact.

I lead a “portfolio life” as an investor speaker and trusted advisor to organizations around the world. I bring imagination empathy and courage to the boardroom helping leaders harness innovation for both agility and scale. I stay at the forefront of what’s next as an Operating Partner at Good Growth Capital investing in early-stage complex science and technology ventures. 

Some things I’m proud of:

• Worked with TED to conceive launch and host the first ever TEDx event
• Pioneered an innovation center model at MIT expanded it at USC advised the Obama Administration to replicate best practices at federal agencies and universities across the US
• Co-founded and grew award-winning computer telephony pioneer Stylus Innovation through two pivots and an acquisition and played key roles at tech companies in Boston and Silicon Valley leading to two exits
• While board President grew the New England Mountain Bike Association's membership 10x via a new chapter structure and led International Mountain Bicycling Association’s launch of a global affiliate model
• Chaired World Economic Forum's global council for fostering entrepreneurship and spoke several times at Davos
• Reached the final round of MIT’s famed robotics competition—and lost with gracious professionalism
• Built temporary airports in the Black Rock Desert as quartermaster of 88NV
• Won MIT’s Battle of Bands as bassist of Bucket O’ Lunch
• Learned to fly planes teach SCUBA diving mountain bike off 5' rocks & backcountry ski 12k' peaks—and persisted earning my skydiving license despite my parachute failing on jump #8

I credit my thirst for adventure to my parents—both Hungarian refugees—whose idea of family vacation was exploring jungles of Belize. I live and adventure from my home base of L.A.

I'm open to one more paying corporate board role. Reach out! linktr.ee/krisztinaholly

about:
NOTE: Do not use LinkedIn to send me funding proposals. Please direct all funding requests to our Submit Web page. Initial response is usually within a few waking hours. Never more than 2 business days.

 http://rightsidecapital.com/submit/

I've always loved solving interesting problems with cool technologies. This desire naturally led to lots of involvement in technology startups. The next logical step was tackling the problem of getting more innovative companies up and running.

I hypothesized that an "engineering approach" to investing one that combined basic portfolio math and software infrastructure would enable a firm to fund 100s of startup per year. This approach would both increase the ease of delivering investment dollars to high-quality founders and reduce the volatility of investor returns. By having the largest portfolio it would have the most efficient process and the lowest risk.

I founded Right side Capital Management (RSCM) with Stanford friend David Lambert to pursue the idea. After building a quantitative model of the startup funding lifecycle and validating the approach against the available data we started investing in 2012. In our first first year we made 79 investments and had the privilege of working with some of the finest founding teams in the US.

Specialties: Business mathematical and software modeling.
